title: Ternary operator evaluates string as true
symptom: The ternary operator returns unexpected results due to string values being interpreted as boolean. This leads to logic checks failing when they shouldn't.
applies_when: Using ternary operator in a workflow
Variables are set as strings instead of booleans
not_this_if: Variables are correctly set as boolean values
root_cause: In JavaScript, strings that are not empty are truthy, meaning that 'false' as a string will evaluate to true. This can cause logical checks to behave unexpectedly when using ternary operators.
fix_steps: Change the variable assignment from string to boolean
Use 'OrdersMenu' without quotes in the ternary operator
agent_instruction: Ensure that the variables are set as boolean values instead of strings.
